brand.name: Brooklyn Spectacles
brand.vertical: Local & service — independent eyewear retail (DTC + brick-and-mortar)
brand.tagline: Beautifully crafted frames inspired by Brooklyn

offer.what: Handcrafted independent eyeglass frames (optical + sunglasses) and children's eyewear ('Little Spectacles'), made with Italian acetate and sold DTC online plus flagship store at 291 Bedford Ave, Williamsburg, Brooklyn. Also distributed through authorized US/Canada retailers.
offer.coreOutcome: Wearing distinctive, quality-crafted frames that signal creative taste and Brooklyn identity — a clear step above mass-market chain eyewear.
offer.pricePoint: $195–$225 for standard optical frames; $95 on sale; $495 for designer collaborations (e.g. Issey Miyake). One-off purchase; no subscription.
offer.commitment: One-time purchase. Repeat only when updating prescription, replacing worn frames, or adding a second pair.
leadMagnets: Sale collection (up to 50% off selected frames)

audience.icp: Style-conscious urban creatives and professionals, 25–45, in Brooklyn/NYC metro and major US/Canada cities. They care about craftsmanship and independent brands, distrust mass-market chains, and treat their glasses as a fashion accessory as much as a vision tool.
painPoints: Generic, boring frames from LensCrafters / Warby Parker feel like everyone else's
Cheap hinges and thin acetate that warp or break within a year
Hard to find quality independent eyewear without flying to Europe
Online buying anxiety — can't try before you buy
segments: Online DTC buyers across US and Canada
Parents buying kids' frames (Little Spectacles line)
Gift buyers (gift cards, gifting a pair)

salesCycle.length: Same-day (in-store) to 3–7 days (online browse-to-checkout). Repeat purchases may be annual or longer as prescriptions change.
salesCycle.trial: No home trial program found. In-store try-on available at flagship. Online buyers rely on photos, measurements, and return/exchange policy.
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve online checkout (Shopify) or in-store walk-in purchase. No sales call required.
salesCycle.topObjection: Price ($195–$225 frame-only feels premium vs. fast-fashion eyewear) and online fit uncertainty — 'What if they don't suit my face / fit my prescription needs?'

ahaMoment: The moment a customer puts on their finished pair — either in-store during the fitting or the first time they open the delivery box — and the craftsmanship, fit, and compliments confirm the price was worth it.

retention.recurring: Not inherently recurring. Natural repeat cycle is 1–3 years (prescription update, frame replacement, or wanting a new look). Sunglasses represent an incremental second-pair purchase.
retention.expansion: Second pair upsell (optical + sun), children's frames for parents already in the brand, gift cards driving new-customer acquisition, limited-edition and collaboration drops.
retention.churnRisk: Single-purchase mentality — customers buy once and don't think about the brand again until their next prescription change. No loyalty program or ongoing reason to engage.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding: no evidence of a post-purchase sequence reinforcing quality, care instructions, or second-pair cross-sell
Lead-Magnet Nurture: email list signup with no known nurture flow for non-buyers
Post-Purchase Care: no frame care, lens cleaning, or adjustment reminder sequence visible
Value Newsletter: no editorial or community content series found
Renewal / Re-engagement: no 'time for a new pair?' or prescription-anniversary trigger
Referral / Advocacy: hashtag UGC encouraged (#LoveMyBKS) but no structured referral or review-request email flow
Win-Back: no re-engagement campaign for lapsed buyers evident
toneVoice: Confident and craft-proud without being pretentious. Brooklyn indie energy — grounded in local creative culture, conversational, celebrates the intersection of artistry and everyday wear. Avoids corporate polish; leans into place, craft, and community.
compliance: Standard retail / CAN-SPAM / CASL (ships to Canada). No healthcare/HIPAA exposure — brand sells frames only, not vision care services or prescriptions.
